item.title: B-Sides and mostly lame stuff.
item.dc:creator: RestingVikingFace

item.description: Ingredients: 1.5 lbs (680g) sirloin steak, cut into 1-inch cubes. Salt and pepper, to taste. 2 tablespoons olive oil. 4 tablespoons unsalted butter, divided. 6 cloves garlic, minced. 1 teaspoon fresh thyme leaves. 1 teaspoon fresh rosemary leaves, chopped. 1 teaspoon fresh parsley, chopped. 1 tablespoon butter. 13 cloves garlic, minced. 1 cup heavy cream. - ½ cup grated Parmesan cheese. Salt and pepper, to taste. 1 teaspoon Italian seasoning 2 - In a large skillet over medium-high heat, add olive oil and 2 tablespoons of butter. Once the butter has melted, add the steak in a single layer. Cook the steak bites for about 3-4 minutes, turning to brown all sides until they reach the desired doneness. Remove the steak from the skillet and set aside. In the same skillet, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter. Add the minced garlic, thyme, rosemary, and parsley, sautéing for about 1 minute until fragrant. Return the steak bites to the skillet, toss to coat them in the garlic herb butter, and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside. In a separate skillet, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Pour in the heavy cream and bring it to a gentle simmer. Stir occasionally. Add the grated Parmesan cheese and Italian seasoning, stirring until the cheese has melted and the sauce is smooth. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Allow the sauce to simmer for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it thickens slightly. Pour the Parmesan cream sauce over the steak bites in the skillet or serve it on the side as a dipping sauce. Garnish with additional parsley, if desired, and serve immediately.

item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Saturday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
as is tradition
i guess
mmm hmm
what else could it mean?
i think that's a sloth
i've yet to know a chick who was into daggers/knives
wow that looks awful
yes it is not friday
what does that even mean?
i'm sorry what
don't say harvested please
HELLO MR THOMPSON
brings me back
really kind of sums it up
hope they are better than the lemon party
lots of good things here
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!

item.title: The question that my “undecided” neighbor asks shouldn’t be “why do you hate him?” I should be asking her why she doesn’t. She says she hates Harris’s laugh. I hate Trump’s fascism, felonies, and rapes.

item.title: the harpy's meme library: volume 65 (off to join the skeleton war)
item.description:
Hello and welcome to another meme dump, my imgur friends. I gotta say, it has been a week. Let us relax and enjoy some amusing images together.
I love all those things except sweet tea, that shit is nasty and you know it. Fight me, southerners.
He sure does, lucky for him he's SO CUTE and such a GOOD BOY
Yeah, it takes a lot of time and effort and shit to get that makeup right, I only bring out the full goth look for special occasions
I sure loved this show
Everything I know is against my will
Aww, beans
@CodyBurkett has no power over me because I happen to live in a part of California that rivals Arizona in terms of lethal heat. But the rest of you better watch out.
I'm the one on the right
I've come a long ways from my youth, I just spent the last few days in the Sims building a lot in painstaking detail even in the dark corners people probably won't even look. It's beautiful and I am proud of myself, easily the coolest thing I've ever made
Yeah I'm one of those people who needs vision insurance but doesn't have it, so like, seeing is fucking expensive, but I gotta see, you know? RIP my money
okie dokie
fuck yeah All right, that's all for this dump, my imgur friends. Until next time, don't forget to be awesome.

item.title: "There's some good in this world, Mr. Frodo, and it's worth fighting for"
item.description:
I work at a post-acute/rehabilitation center, lots of older folks. Recently we've been having a series of residents getting CoVID-19 infections, and so all nursing staff (yours truly included) have been working hard to follow precautions and wearing PPE when entering quarantined rooms. So you can imagine how pissed I feel over getting it myself. Eggothy Noodlebert, though, knows I'm not doing well, and has been actively comforting me during the last couple of days. I often joke that there's no thoughts in that head of his, but truly it's filled with nothing but love and care.
Edit 1: oh, hi again, Front Page! Edit 2: quite of people here seem to appreciate/are fascinated by Egg's name, so I figured I should let you all know his full, current name is Eggothy Thomas Noodlebert, Esquire; he shall accumulate more parts to his name as time passes and we witness more of his full personality. Edit 3: shameless Front Page plug, if you guys like sci-fi and fantasy you should check out my recent posts about my sci-fintasy setting; especially with how much people complain about seeing "too much politics", it wouldn't hurt to take a peek now would it? :3 1) https://imgur.com/gallery/lUBDfbU 2) https://imgur.com/gallery/bMUkPcE

item.dc:creator: LitterboxComics

item.title: It's been a hell of a week...
item.description:
Monday morning my wife took herself to the hospital because she was leaking fluid, she got admitted for preterm labor, and possible preeclampsia. The doctors planned to keep her on magnesium and steroids and to stall the labor until Wednesday. Tuesday during an ultrasound they find a huge mass in my son's chest that is squishing his heart and lungs (Google CPAM if you're curious). I miraculously finished my training at work early and arrived at the hospital shortly after they found out and I learned the news. Within half an hour my wife and I were being life flighted out of state to a hospital with a pediatric surgeon who deals with this issue regularly.
The flight was about an hour. Luckily it was in a fixed wing plane so no helicopter. It was still super cramped.
The plane is fully equipped with all the necessary life support and they were checking our baby's heart rate every 15 minutes.
Luckily my wife managed to get some sleep on the flight.
Pardon the selfie, but I did get to wear this nifty headset. Before we could land we had to wait for a passenger plane to make an emergency landing because they had an engine catch fire. Can you guess what kind of plane that was? If you guessed it was a Boeing, you're 100% correct!
My wife is a goddamn champion, and I couldn't be more proud of her. The continued with the medicine at the new hospital so she went through three days of off and on contractions while being on magnesium, having her vitals checked every few hours until they officially started an induction on Wednesday night to get us into active labor. All the while she gets no sleep and is still worried about me managing to get a decent amount of rest whenever I could. 17 more hours of active labor and our little boy was born at 5 lbs 1 oz.
We got to hold him for exactly one minute before I cut the cord and they passed him to the NICU.
Once he was stabilized they brought him over soy wife and I could see him before he was transported to the children's hospital across the way.
I was able to go with them but my wife was still recovering and had to wait. He spent Wednesday night and Thursday there getting around the clock care. Friday morning he went in for surgery. They removed the full upper lobe of his right lung where the mass was.
This was taken Friday after surgery. Everything went as well as we could have hoped. He is already improving so quickly and his heart lungs moved right back and the lungs inflated like they should. His vitals keep getting better and it looks like he's going to be a perfectly healthy boy eventually with no long lasting health issues. I haven't been able to hold him, and we won't be able to until he's off the ventilator, and that's likely not going to happen before I have to go back home for work. We're incredibly fortunate that my wife has maternity leave and FMLA so she'll be able to stay out here for the four to six weeks he'll need to stay in the NICU, and we got a room at the Ronald McDonald house so food and a place to sleep won't be an issue. I'll be able to stay out here for another week before I make the 6 hour drive back home. I'm dreading leaving them, my wife and I haven't been apart for more than a few days since we started dating, but we can't have lots of animals at home and with the medical costs of everything looming on the horizon, I can't afford to lose the job I just got. (For context I got laid off in March, normally I'm a Software and Data Engineer, but the job market has been a nightmare. Unemployment insurance ran out and I had to get a job delivering for Amazon to keep us afloat. I do have an interview next week for a super nice remote data job so fingers crossed the delivery gig doesn't last too long ) Anyway, this is probably going to be the longest month of my life. It's going to suck. However, I'm so grateful for all the care we've gotten, how fortunate we are that he's already recovering so well, so thankful that we have a place for my wife to stay for free while our boy is in the hospital, and talking with other families at the hospital, I know there's so much worse things that could happen to a child during development. One quick surgery and a few weeks in the NICU don't seem that bad by comparison. TLDR: son came early and needed surgery, but everything is going super well and he's going to make a full recovery
item.dc:creator: Necridos

item.title: A visit to the Filming Locations of numerous Horror Movies
item.description:
SCREAM - Stu Macher's house is located at 3871 Tomales Petaluma Road in Tomales, California
THE EXORCIST - Built in 1895, the stairway connects Prospect and 36th Streets with M Street, NW in Georgetown, Washington DC
THE FOG - The Point Reyes Lighthouse in Marin County, California
GHOSTBUSTERS - "Firehouse, Hook & Ladder Company 8" is a real New York City fire station, located at 14 North Moore Street at its intersection with Varick Street in the Tribeca
HALLOWEEN - The Myers House, located at 1000 Mission Street in South Pasadena, California.
THE LOST BOYS (1987) The railroad trestle bridge to which Michael is taken to explore his new powers is the Iron Horse Trailhead, at Exit 170 of I-5 in Valencia, just north of Santa Clarita, itself north of LA.
THE BLOB (1958) - The Colonial Theatre is located (and still operating in) Phoenixville, Pennsylvania. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GODDLgM1gKo Built in 1903, Mary Pickford visited the theatre in 1915 and Harry Houdini held a performance here in 1917 (freeing himself from a safe). It played mainly silent films until "The Jazz Singer" in 1928. THE BLOB made the small theater forever famous, and helped to keep it preserved as a historic landmark.
HOCUS POCUS - The Ropes Mansion in Salem, Massachusetts served as the exterior for Allison's house in the 1993 Disney classic.
Old Burial Hill in Marblehead was also utilized in the opening of the movie. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ogNGgpvWnAg
THE TEXAS CHAINSAW MASSACRE - Located at 1073 TX-304 in Bastrop, Texas is the famous gas station BBQ where the characters stop for directions at the start of the movie https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SAcVBEcM_zg
THE TEXAS CHAINSAW MASSACRE - Now transformed into a quaint little restaurant. Originally found in Round Rock's Quick Hill, Texas... the building was moved in 1998, to avoid demolition by developers, to Kingsland, Texas (60 miles away).
A NIGHTMARE ON ELM STREET - 1428 North Genesee Avenue in Los Angeles
THE HAUNTING (1963) - The Ettington Park Hotel (in Warwickshire, England) served as the exterior for HILL HOUSE... a film based on Shirley Jackson's ghostly novel about the investigation of a haunted house deep in New England https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=evjqH468-H4 The chapel at the estate was also used for filming the 1980 Disney horror movie "The Watcher in the Woods" (an area of the house that was truely believed to be haunted). Vol 1 of Horror Film Locations - https://imgur.com/gallery/rh1jn0V
THE OMEN - All Saints Church, Fulham (London, England) - Just north of Bishop's Park along the River Thames, where they also filmed.
Father Brennan (Patrick Troughton) meets his gruesome end for saying too much about Damian just outside the church https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y1AKp7mz1g4
THE EVIL DEAD - What remains of the old cabin in Morristown, Tennessee (destroyed in a fire after the original movie was filmed there)
The foundation and the chimney are all that remains.
THE AMITYVILLE HORROR - The house used for filming in the 1979 movie is located in Toms River, New Jersey The original house the film (and book) is based on is located at 12 Ocean Avenue in Amityville, NY (apparently the best haunted places are at Ocean Avenues... ghosts must like the view)
IN THE MOUTH OF MADNESS - The Cathedral of the Transfiguration, a former Slovak-Greek Catholic Church located in Markham, Ontario (Canada). https://i.imgur.com/wIqmRo3.jpg The exterior of the cathedral was used in the 1995 John Carpenter film, where it was as portrayed the "Black Church" integral to the evil that lives in Hobb's End, New Hampshire https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FeWm_jk7G80
ROSEMARY'S BABY - The Dakota Apartments, 1 West 72nd Street at Central Park West (the same location where John Lennon was murdered in 1980).
FRIDAY THE 13th - A real camp located in Hardwick Township, New Jersey was used for filming
Orava Castle in Slovakia, where "NOSFERATU" was filmed in 1922
THE SHINING - The Timberline Lodge, on the south side of Mount Hood in Oregon
HOUSE (1986) - Located at 329 Melrose Avenue, Monrovia, California https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9TSRT-cl-y8
NIGHT OF THE LIVING DEAD (1968) - The graveyard is located in Evans City, Pennsylvania
THE OMEN - Pyrford Court, a 21-acre estate located in Woking, Surrey (England) was used as the filming location for the home of U.S. Ambassador Thorn (played by Gregory Peck). NSFW - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CMCZ0ERKJtU
BRONSON CAVES is located in the southwest section of Griffith Park near the north end of Canyon Drive. It's served a popular filming location for hundreds of films and shows throughout the years. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oA6_my28pj4 Just a few horror films that have utilized it include: ARMY OF DARKNESS, INVASION OF THE BODY SNATCHERS (1956), IT CONQUERED THE WORLD, WHITE ZOMBIE, THE VAMPIRE BAT, EARTH VS THE SPIDER, NIGHT OF THE BLOOD BEAST, TREMORS 4, CABIN FEVER https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bronson_Canyon#Media_filmed_or_set_in_the_canyon
HELLRAISER - The home of Frank Cotton is located 87 Dollis Hill Lane, in London, England
As it was depicted in the 1987 film
THE BLAIR WITCH PROJECT - Seneca Creek State Park, Gaithersburg, Maryland
Coffin Rock
INTERVIEW WITH THE VAMPIRE (1994) - The Oak Alley Plantation in Vacherie, St. James Parish, Louisiana https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7JPLVQEqE60
POLTERGEIST - 4267 Roxbury Street, Simi Valley, California (NO, it didn't actually get sucked into the Ghost dimension after filming) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9eZgEKjYJqA Vol 1 - https://imgur.com/gallery/rh1jn0V Vol 2 - https://imgur.com/gallery/94xsIeY
THE RING - From the opening scene of the 2002 remake, located in Hancock Park (413 S. McCadden Place, Los Angeles)
THE BIRDS - The school house located in real-life Bodega Bay, California
AN AMERICAN WEREWOLF IN LONDON - Crickadarn Village, Wales served as the location for "The Slaughtered Lamb" Pub and the fictional surrounding village at the start of the film
THE SENTINEL - A quaint brownstone apartment in Brooklyn that turns out to be a gateway to Hell https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z0YdJNeNXLE Located at 10 Montague Terrace, in Brooklyn Heights (New York City)
Located 18 miles away in Downingtown, Pennsylvania along the Lincoln Highway, is "Chef's Diner" (formerly the Cadillac Diner) where the climactic scene in the BLOB was filmed. New owners took over and eliminated any assocation to the movie... and shortly thereafter went out of business. Sort of a stark comparison between this place and the Colonial. EDIT: I just discovered it's once again been taken over by new management, and has since been reopened - https://www.dtowndiner.com/
BLACK CHRISTMAS (1974) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M-bRPUF-xb4 Considered one of the very first "Slasher" films that redefined the Horror genre (released 4 years before HALLOWEEN), 6 Clarendon Crescent in Toronto, Ontario served as the filming location for the sorority house where the killer goes on a holiday killing spree.
Soldiers' Tower at the University of Toronto Almost ten years later, director Bob Clark would direct a very different kind of Christmas-themed movie, compared to this early slasher. "A Christmas Story" https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cfjEZ88NHBw
Sidney Prescott's scenic house (built in 1995, just a year before filming took place) is located at 1820 Calistoga Road, Santa Rosa, California.
SCREAM - 7420 Sonoma Mountain Rd, in Glen Ellen, California. Wes Craven and Marianne Maddalena personally chose this secluded house as the setting for the film's opening scene.
THE LEGEND OF HELL HOUSE - Filmed at Wykehurst Place, a Gothic Revival mansion in Bolney, West Sussex, England https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pacLdQdJJpY
Iranian writer and filmmaker Ebrahim Golestan owned and lived in Wykehurst Place from 1984 until his death in August of 2023
A NIGHTMARE ON ELM STREET 3: DREAM WARRIORS - Royce Hall at the UCLA campus
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ThN7KZc4KR0
HALLOWEEN - "The Hedge", from one of the film's most iconic scenes, is located on 1019 and 1025 Montrose Ave, South Pasadena (California)
THRILLER - The house seen at the end of the 1983 music video, where Ola Ray takes shelter after the zombie dance scene. Located in 1345 Carroll Ave, Los Angeles.
I Know What You Did Last Summer (1999) Julie James' house - 209 Short St. Southport, North Carolina
Southport Muscle, a gym in the film, has now become a cafe - 106 Yacht Basin Dr. Southport, NC
Southport Community Theatre - 111 N. Howe St. Southport, NC. 28461 (Amuzu Theatre)
PRINCE OF DARKNESS - The Japanese Union Church, located 120 Judge John Aiso Street in Downtown Los Angeles
NIGHT OF THE DEMON (known as "Curse of the Demon" in the US) - Brocket Hall in Hertfordshire, England https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4yYoEShoNzk
SCREAM 2 - 2186 East Crary Street in Altadena, California was used as the sorority house where Ghostface makes his next kill. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0De1lC1vViI
SCREAM 2 (and THRILLER) - The Rialto Theater in South Pasadena, California (originally opened in 1925)
Culzean Castle served as the estate of Lord Summerisle (played by Christopher Lee)
THE WICKER MAN - the movie was filmed all over Scotland in towns such as Stranraer, Gatehouse of Fleet, Newton Stewart, Kirkcudbright, and here in Plockton
St Ninian's Cave in Physgill Glen, Whithorn, Dumfries and Galloway - It served as the location for the climax of the film
These stumps are all that remain of the Wicker Man itself (constructed in 1972)
What it looks like today after it was rebuilt/remodeled
THE 'BURBS - Colonial Street on the Universal Studios Lot has seen a lot of film history on it, even before the 1989 horror comedy was filmed there. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4q4_TAHyOYI
What was left of the "Klopek house" after filming https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qg7RpL3kTBk
HOUSE ON HAUNTED HILL - Frank Lloyd Wright’s iconic "Ennis House" in Los Angeles, California
THE DEAD ZONE (1983) - Filming took place in and around Niagara Falls, Ontario, Canada (aka the good side). This is the SCREAMING TUNNEL, an old abandoned drainage tunnel that's earned the local reputation of being a haunted spot... legends of a girl who burned to death in there and whose SCREAMS it's said can still be heard there. The spot was used in the movie as the scene of a grisly murder committed by a serial killer in Castle Rock, Maine https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qxJp_VQ5VUk
17 Byron Street, located in Niagara-on-the-Lake served as the exterior of Johnny Smith (Christopher Walken)'s house. The town was also used for filming locations in "Trapped in Paradise" (1994), "The Ref" (1994) and "Canadian Bacon" (1995)
The Gazebo (the scene of another grisly murder) was built by the crew in Queen's Royal Park, and later donated it to Niagara-on-the-Lake after filming was over.
Court House Theatre in Orono, Ontario served as the exterior location for the climactic scene where Walken has to assassinate Jed Bartlet to prevent his becoming President and starting World War 3. The interior was actually filmed at another location, the real Orono townhall.
HALLOWEEN - The Wallace house (where the majority of Michael Myers' killing spree takes place) is located at1537 Orange Grove Ave, Los Angeles. Extensive remodeling makes it hard to spot unless you know what you're looking for.
STEPHEN KING'S ROSE RED - Thornwood Castle in Lakewood, Washington (40 miles to the south of Seattle where the film is set) served as the exterior filming location for the 2002 TV movie https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bTfgbzzCbug Built in 1910, from the brick of a dismantled 15th-century house imported all the way from England. https://i.imgur.com/XaO4Ams.jpg
The estate was also used for filming the prequel "The Diary of Ellen Rimbauer" https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wyLRuAI_leg The 2020 Netflix miniseries "The Haunting of Bly Manor" used Thornwood as inspiration for its eponymous haunted house, created with CGI while filming in nearby Vancouver, Washington.
DAMIEN: OMEN II - Indian Point Manor served as the exterior for the home of William Holden and Lee Grant's characters. Located at 1057 Everett Rd, Eagle River, Wisconsin
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=k4h8ZidNYjM
THE FRIGHTENERS - Erskine College, Wellington (New Zealand) was a a Catholic girls' boarding school (Convent of the Sacred Heart at Island Bay) long rumored to be haunted before its closure in 1985. It was used by director Peter Jackson for filming in 1996. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lKWiQLwr9Xs All but the chapel was finally demolished in 2018 after decades of sitting empty.
Jackson on location with Michael J. Fox. Location shooting primarily included Wellington and three weeks spent in Lyttelton. Interior scenes were compiled at Camperdown Studios in Miramar.
Guildford Cathedral, Surrey (England) Where Damian has a violent reaction to his parents attempt to take him to church https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LO3KlIShoHo
The ballroom scene actually took place in the front lobby https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dZXsZamnX3o
GHOSTBUSTERS - The Millenium Biltmore Hotel in downtown Los Angeles filled in for the fictional "Sedgewick Hotel" in NYC, where the Ghostbusters captured their first ghost... Slimer
Wes Craven's THE PEOPLE UNDER THE STAIRS - 2215 South Harvard Blvd, Los Angeles (California) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gx4jKIzvVUo Fun fact, the house is actually located right across the street from...
It was also used in many other films: The Brides of Dracula (1962), The Reptile (1966), The Plague of the Zombies (1966), The Old Dark House (1963), Witchcraft (1964), And Now the Screaming Starts! (1973), Vampyres (1974), the mystery farce Murder by Death (1976), and the Peter Cook and Dudley Moore comedy, "The Hound of the Baskervilles" (1978)
The Oakley Court Hotel (in Berkshire, England) has served as the exterior for THE ROCKY HORROR PICTURE SHOW
CANDYMAN - The infamous Cabrini-Green projects in Chicago, Illinois served as the setting and filming location for the movie. None of it remains today, it was finally torn down... only to be replaced by gentrification. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sT1QR_VuV8s "Exterior, hallway and stairway scenes were actually filmed for a few days in the housing projects, though the producers had to make a deal with the ruling gang members to put them in the movie as extras to ensure the cast and crew's safety during filming. Even with this arrangement, a sniper put a bullet through the production van on the last day of filming, though no one was injured"
Abbeville, Louisiana served as the filming location for the fictional "Arborville, California". https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HQqV55GayNA TRUE DETECTIVE also filmed there during its first season.
The cast and crew of approximately 150 staying at a local Travelodge, but due to the large amount of night shooting the cast often slept during the day. On their off days, they watched videos at the hotel and ate crawfish, a popular item of local cuisine.
SESSION 9 - The Danvers State Hospital in Danvers, Massachusetts (what remains of it after a demolition in 2006 and a fire in '07). A former lunatic asylum that served as the setting for the 2001 film. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4s-5eJq47bY
Historically, the hospital also served as the basis for the H.P. Lovecraft setting of Arkham, Massachusetts (with the Arkham Sanitarium featured in "The Thing on the Doorstep"). This in turn inspired writer Dennis O'Neil in creating what has become an iconic part of the Batman mythos ... the infamous Gotham setting known as "ARKHAM ASYLUM", where all of the insane members of the caped crusader's rogues gallery were locked away https://i.imgur.com/oMk9q2x.jpg
LAKE PLACID - Shawnigan Lake, British Columbia was the filming location for this 1999 horror film... about as far as you can get from Maine where the film was set.
It was shut down in 2018 for... well for being CREEPS. Hazing incidents and sexual assaults got the fraternity suspended.
NIGHT OF THE CREEPS - The fictional Kappa Delta sorority house where the majority of the film takes place used the FIJI fraternity in Los Angeles, California. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_PmPuYg6nqM
SUSPIRIA - A perfect reconstruction of the Haus zum Walfisch in Freiburg (Baden-Württemberg, Germany) was used as the facade of the Dance Academy. The majority of the film was shot at De Paoli studios in Rome, whilst the exterior shots were filmed in locations all around Munich and Bavaria... including the headquarters of BMW.
GRAVEYARD SHIFT - The historic Bartlett Mill (the oldest woolen yarn mill in America), located in Harmony, Maine
ROALD DAHL'S THE WITCHES - The Headland Hotel (located in Newquay, Cornwall, UK) was used as the filming location for the 1990 film
ROGER CORMAN'S THE UNDEAD - The Spadena House (aka the Witch's House) is located in Beverly Hills, California. It was built in 1921 by art film director Harry Oliver, who played a major role in Storybook architecture. It served as the offices and dressing rooms for Irvin Willat's silent film studio in Culver City. After the studio closed, a family by the name of Spadena moved in. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5t1CtVdODj8
GHOST STORY (1981) - Starring Melvyn Douglas, John Houseman, Douglas Fairbanks Jr. and Fred Astaire. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=p2UlwI-7etc The haunted house that played an integral part to the film is located at 60 Smith Bridge Rd, in Saratoga Springs, New York This is how it looked in the 80s and 90s, here it is today - https://i.imgur.com/cHeONvO.jpg
How the Saratoga Springs residence looks today
The movie holds the trifecta of being the final film roles for Astaire, Douglas and Fairbanks Jr. The crew added a touch of decay to the house, as well as a tower to the fifth window
MANOS: THE HANDS OF FATE - Not much of the filming location in El Paso, Texas still remains
El Paso's Scenic Drive, from the opening scene of the film... the very very very VERY long opening scene of the film
The Master's bed
PSYCHO - The Universal Studio lot (in Burbank, California), is where the Bates Motel and adjacent "Psycho" house are located
THE EXORCIST - 3600 Prospect Street, Georgetown, Washington DC served as the filming location for the exterior of the Regan house (the interiors were shot in Hell's Kitchen, NYC) https://i.imgur.com/gbBhxsj.png A substantial false side was built onto the existing house just to create the most iconic shot of the movie https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mRxTgasJUdo
THE CONJURING - Located at 405 Canetuck Road in Currie, North Carolina
THE BLOOD ON SATAN'S CLAW - The ruins of St. James’ Old Church in Bix Bottom (Oxfordshire, England). Abandoned since 1825. The church had been used for filming in a prior Hammer horror film, "The Witches" (1966). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_c15CwbMlq0
1336 North Harper Avenue, West Hollywood served as Cotton Weary's home in the opening scene https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bYi-rmHfrP8
SCREAM 3 - The Parmour Estate is a historic residence in 1923 Micheltorena Street, Silver Lake (California). It served as the setting for the film's finale, and was also used as the setting for HALLOWEEN: H20 (filmed at the property for the fictional "Hillcrest Academy"). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fbt9_q0Pjr0
PET SEMATARY - 303 Point Road in Hancock, Maine
PET SEMATARY - Mount Hope Cemetery in Bangor, Maine
THE SHINING (1997) - The TV adapt of Stephen King's novel (made due to his dissatisfaction of the changes Stanley Kubrick did in his movie version) was filmed at the Stanley Hotel in Estes Park, Colorado. While the TV movie recieved very poor critical reception (when compared to Kubrick's version), the filming location was fitting since the Stanley Hotel was where King originally came up with the idea for the novel... while staying there with his wife Tabitha in 1974.
THE SILENCE OF THE LAMBS (and RED DRAGON) - The exterior of the Western Center (an actual mental hospital) near Canonsburg, Pennsylvania, served as the setting for "Baltimore State Hospital for the Criminally Insane". It was shutdown in 1998 after years of lawsuits filed by disability advocates, the old hospital had an insidious reputation for neglect and abuse of its patients. It was completely demolished in 2011.
THE CONVENT (1999) - 2218 South Harvard St, Los Angeles. A historical landmark that was used in a film about girls trapped in a former nunnery filled with demons https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1DLkItGalNc
The remake went with a vastly different shooting location... El Hammam, Morocco (near Ouarzazate). Ironically, the sets built there are still standing unlike those back in the US https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3fdt2mVGyH8
THE HILLS HAVE EYES - Victorville, California, located in the Mojave Desert, was the primary filming location https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pzLfsql1Yvc
PHANTASM (and BURNT OFFERINGS) - Dunsmuir House in Oakland, California
TREMORS - Nothing of the actual sets remain, they were quickly built in under 2 months and then destroyed (during and after filming). Out in the middle of nowhere, near Cactus Flats Rd. in Olancha, California. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=czxjeKf6tJE Famous Horror Film Locations, Vol 1 - https://imgur.com/gallery/rh1jn0V Vol 2 - https://imgur.com/gallery/94xsIeY Vol 3 - https://imgur.com/gallery/crr63Oi
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SgcYRu8kvD8
The exact GPS location: https://i.imgur.com/FpSyzgm.png https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gnqPYTOzc38
This would be the rock Val, Earl and Rhonda get stuck on... it's located some miles away at Movie Flat Rd. in Lone Pine, California (where a LOT of movies have filmed there): https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_films_shot_in_Lone_Pine
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VxLyhOFmdxE
Roche Rock Hermitage in Roch, Cornwall (United Kingdom) served as another setting for a demonic death scene.
OMEN 3: THE FINAL CONFLICT - The finale was filmed at the Fountains Abbey near Ripon, North Yorkshire https://i.imgur.com/FYfEqvP.jpg A tour of the Abbey: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tVZZ3cbUxBc
Treffry Viaduct in Luxulyan, Cornwall was where a hilariously adorable death took place in the movie https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_pNO4sp-UHw An overhead shot: https://i.imgur.com/srUF1Oi.jpg
637 S. Lucerne Blvd. Los Angeles (California) Serving as the house of one of the main characters, the location has been utilized in other films and shows like: "Willard", "Ben", "Elvira: Mistress of the Dark", "Witchboard" and an episode of "Tales from the Crypt" (Television Terror).
WAXWORK - 255 S. Rossmore Ave. (Los Angeles, California) was used as the exterior for the bloody wax museum https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LbdebLeSOr8
SATURDAY THE 14TH - 1190 West Adams Boulevard
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Yf_0-GpNOi8
Lyons NY was were the majority of shooting took place, downtown with the Wayne County Courthouse (at 26 Church Street) and the nearby school (at 144 Main Street) which served the centerpiece of the movie... where a ghostly murder takes place
THE LADY IN WHITE - Filming took place in upstate NY, in and around Rochester (where director Alex Rocco grew up and where the local legend that inspired the film is based). https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dzBK95kyxNg Coye Cemetary in South Bristol, NY (overlooking Canandaigua Lake) is a scenic spot, even for a graveyard.
THE LAST HOUSE ON THE LEFT (1972) - Wes Craven's very first movie. Filming took place in Westport, Conneticut. Many of the most horrific scenes took place in and around the Poplar Cemetary (located at 287 Wilton Rd, Westport) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ib7LR6_SxdY
The pond is also right next to the cemetary.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HSgWFuyezE0
THE GATE - Filmed at 150 Curtis Crescent in King City, Ontario (Canada)
DAWN OF THE DEAD (1978) - Monroeville , Pennsylvania (just east of Pittsburg) is where the iconic Mall featured in the film is located. George A. Romero shot it at night after the mall closed, from 11pm until dawn at 7am (the automatic music that kicked on in the morning signaled to the crew that filming was over) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y_EviQj5tvA
https://i.imgur.com/2rj97Om.jpg
All that remains of the footbridge, the last visual feature that remained from how the mall looked in '78. It was removed and (thankfully) relocated to the Heinz History Center in Pittsburg
Made in tribute to the filming, one of the few stores still open in the mall.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rpH4GkSsXg4
HALLOWEEN 4: THE RETURN OF MICHAEL MYERS - 509 3rd Ave. Salt Lake City, Utah The film was shot all around Utah (Copperton, Honeyville, Midvale, Ogden, SLC and Stockton)
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KeSAFGWzft8
THE RETURN OF THE LIVING DEAD - 698 Moulton Avenue near Downtown Los Angeles
THE CRAFT - A pivotal scene at the beach was filmed at spot along the 35000 Pacific Coast Hwy. (in Malibu, California) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sXw6P3uIpWE
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SxEqB--5ToI
INVASION OF THE BODY SNATCHERS - The 2748 Westshire Drive/2823 Pelham Place Stairs were used for filming one of the most thrilling scenes in the movie. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dLvWRNX2IJI
SCREAM 4 - Wes Craven's final film, with many of its locations shot in Michigan. The house of Jill Prescott can be found at 1005 Lincoln Avenue in Ann Arbor
The finale at Kirby's house takes place at 19223 Clement Road in Northville, Michigan
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JKRtyVLWV-E
item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.title: Oh they loved that!
item.description: They long to return to THOSE good old days...
item.dc:creator: BlueVoter

item.title: Weaponised geese
item.dc:creator: Odoyyoxigoyxc

item.title: mushroom potato soup recipe!
item.description: https://www.triedandtruerecipe.com/easy-mushroom-potato-soup/ 8 ounces cremini mushrooms 3.5 ounces beech mushrooms 1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil 1 yellow onion, peeled and diced 1 parsnip, peeled and small-diced 5 Yukon gold potatoes, peeled and medium-diced 1 teaspoon garlic powder 4 sprigs of thyme or 1 teaspoon dry thyme 6 cups vegetable broth or water ¼ teaspoon white pepper 1 cup elbow pasta 2 cups whole milk ¼ cup heavy cream, optional ⅓ cup loosely packed parsley, minced, plus more for serving Garlic oil optional, for serving Salt and pepper Cook the mushrooms: Place a large pot on the stovetop and turn the heat to medium. Add the cremini mushrooms and cook, turning occasionally, to let them release their liquid. It will take 8-10 minutes. They will be squeaky in the pot as you turn them. Sauté the vegetables: Once any liquid in the pot has evaporated, add the olive oil and the beech mushrooms, onion, and parsnip. Cook for 5–7 minutes until the mushrooms turn golden brown. Season with salt, pepper, and garlic powder. Add the potatoes and place the thyme sprigs on top. Pour in the broth and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to low. Add salt and white pepper and simmer for 30–40 minutes or until the potatoes are fork-tender. Once the potatoes are tender, scoop out and discard the spent thyme sprigs. Cook the elbow pasta: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the elbows until al dente. Drain and set aside. Temper the milk: Scoop 1 cup of broth from the pot and add to a glass measuring cup. Pour in the milk and stir to temper it. Pour the mixture into the pot and continue simmering for 5 minutes. Add the heavy cream if you’d like a richer soup. Finish the soup: Add the cooked elbows to the pot along with the parsley. Taste and add salt and pepper. Simmer for 5 minutes. Turn off the heat. To serve: Ladle the soup into bowls and serve with a pinch of parsley on top. Finish with a drizzle of garlic oil and black pepper on top. Enjoy!
item.dc:creator: triedandtruerecipes
